title: astragalus thracicus subsp. jankae
intro: Astragalus thracicus subsp. jankae, also known as Jankae's milkvetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, native to parts of southeastern Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits associated with Astragalus thracicus subsp. jankae include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its roots and leaves, which can be used in teas, tinctures, or capsules, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
